meaning: adj.悲伤的,使痛苦的,使烦恼的
examples: She witnessed some very distressing scenes.
It is very distressing to see your baby attached to tubes and monitors.
This is particularly distressing because time seems to be a vital factor in the appreciation of all art forms.
Pop music has always been and always will be an aural panacea for distressing times.
The experts said performing CPR in inappropriate cases could result in a distressing and undignified death.
